title: polygala raddiana
intro: Polygala raddiana, also known as Chinese finger root or Yin chiao, is a perennial plant native to China that belongs to the Polygalaceae family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, as well as its ability to modulate the immune system. The health benefits of Polygala raddiana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its use in treating fever and sore throats. In herbal preparations, Polygala raddiana is often used in combination with other herbs, such as Astragalus and licorice root, to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be taken internally to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
